claims.claim_text: Maize-soybean intercropping (one row of soybean between maize rows at 75 x 30 cm) under conservation agriculture produced a land-equivalent ratio above 1.2, indicating a yield advantage over sole cropping.
claims.evidence_text: The LER for both intercropping partterns were above 1.2. We recommend farmers to adopt the intercropping pattern of one row of soybean in between maize row spaced at 75 x 30 cm for better LER, and crop performance.

context.arrangement: one row of soybean between one row of maize

provenance.source_title: Effects of Intercropping on Maize and Soybean Yield Performance, Land Equivalent Ratio, and Maize Leaf Area in Conservation Agriculture

claims.claim_text: Cereal-legume intercropping confers nutrient benefits through cereal-legume interactions, a recognized advantage of intercropping systems.
claims.evidence_text: This is because of the advantages of intercropping, especially nutrient benefits through cereal-legume interactions, alternative sustainable methods to manage biotic stress (pests, diseases, weeds), and crop failure risk management due to erratic weather.

provenance.source_title: Effects of Intercropping on Maize and Soybean Yield Performance, Land Equivalent Ratio, and Maize Leaf Area in Conservation Agriculture
